lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'active', 'notes': 'Secure Digital (SD) memory cards remain the dominant removable flash storage format. SD Express (PCIe) is the current standard; microSD is ubiquitous in smartphones. SD Association actively developing new standards.', 'source': 'General knowledge'}
notes: Subject of study; portable storage for digital info transfer between devices. Key differentiator: interoperability across cameras, camcorders, printers, PCs, PDAs, DVDs. Consumer preferred capacity range 256MB-1GB.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'mature', 'notes': 'Digital camera market is mature (declining consumer, active professional). Known answer applied.', 'source': 'known'}
notes: Primary entry point to digital electronics ecosystem; owned by most focus group participants. Photos transferred primarily via USB cable rather than memory card removal. Explosive growth cited as clear market stimulant.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'discontinued', 'notes': 'Digital video camcorder as a dedicated consumer device is discontinued/absorbed by smartphones. Known answer applied.', 'source': 'known'}
notes: Most commonly owned by families raising children; footage typically transferred to VHS via cable or burned to CD.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'discontinued', 'notes': 'MP3 players as dedicated devices are discontinued. Known answer applied.', 'source': 'known'}
notes: Only a few respondents owned MP3 players in 2003; majority downloaded music to PC then burned CDs. Young people highly likely to store/use music digitally.
Palm Pilots cited; used primarily for work scheduling/directories. Only wireless digital data transfer method in use among respondents was Palm-to-Palm beam.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'discontinued', 'notes': 'Sony Memory Stick is discontinued. Known answer applied.', 'source': 'known'}
notes: Sony Memory Stick users most brand-aware among memory card users; cited as proprietary format competing with SD. SD vs. Memory Stick format war relevant context.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'discontinued', 'notes': 'SmartMedia Card is discontinued. Known answer applied.', 'source': 'known'}
notes: Referenced by one respondent (Boston male) who had SmartMedia with older Mac requiring special card reader; compatibility issues highlighted.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'legacy-supported', 'notes': 'CompactFlash is legacy-supported in niche professional use. Known answer applied.', 'source': 'known'}
notes: Memory card format variety cited as disadvantage by respondents; part of incompatible format landscape that SD aimed to resolve.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'active', 'notes': 'USB ports are active. Known answer applied.', 'source': 'known'}
notes: Primary photo transfer method for most respondents; camera to PC via USB cable. Insufficient USB ports on older computers cited as pain point.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'active', 'notes': 'Home area network evolved to smart home. Known answer applied.', 'source': 'known'}
notes: Only a few respondents had in-home LANs; rarely used for digital data transfer. Wireless data transfer in home was essentially only Palm-to-Palm.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'discontinued', 'notes': 'CD-RW/CD-ROM as primary storage/distribution medium is discontinued. Known answer applied.', 'source': 'known'}
notes: Primary backup/archival medium for photos and music in consumer households; music downloaded then burned to CD; primary competing storage medium to memory cards.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'discontinued', 'notes': 'VHS tape is discontinued. Known answer applied.', 'source': 'known'}
notes: Primary video storage medium in consumer households in 2003; video transferred from camcorder to VHS via cable connection.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'discontinued', 'notes': 'HP Media Center PC was a Windows XP/Vista-era product line (circa 2002-2008) tied to Windows XP Media Center Edition. HP discontinued this specific product category as Windows Media Center itself was removed from Windows 10. The product line no longer exists; replaced by general-purpose home PCs and streaming devices.', 'source': 'https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ZED3iwnKH2U'}
notes: Listed as qualifying device for focus group participation screening; HP-specific digital media PC platform.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'active', 'notes': 'Adobe Photoshop is active. Known answer applied.', 'source': 'known'}
notes: PhotoShop cited by name; photo manipulation and enhancement becoming mainstream hobby; measurable minority of consumers using it.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'discontinued', 'notes': 'P2P music download (Kazaa et al.) is discontinued as a mainstream platform. Known answer applied.', 'source': 'known'}
notes: Kazaa cited by name by San Diego IDI respondent for music downloading. Many younger respondents download music from internet though legality noted with discomfort.

lifecycle_current: {'lifecycle_current': 'active', 'notes': 'iTunes is active (evolved, split into separate apps on Mac). Known answer applied.', 'source': 'known'}
